mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when policy is actively easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline during months with negative 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals weakening household income and demand, which should pressure corporate earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if more than 60% of triggered observations are followed by positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in RSAFS indicates stronger retail spending and faster revenue growth expectations for cyclicals, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases by at least 0.5% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS shocks are not followed by above-median 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the conditional return is unstable across rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both rise together, firms are expanding output and payrolls in tandem, indicating improving real activity that should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone in walk-forward tests, or if triggered observations fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in HOUST after a prior downturn signals a housing-led recovery that improves wealth effects and construction demand, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 8% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST rebounds are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns relative to unconditional baseline, or if the effect disappears once the housing jump is lagged by one month.

mechanism_narrative: Low CPI inflation paired with an elevated policy rate indicates restrictive real rates; when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS stays high, equity multiples should compress and 20-day S&P 500 returns should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS combination does not predict lower 20-day returns than either series separately, or if negative signal episodes do not cluster when policy remains high and inflation slows.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, an unemployment rate near double digits acts as a recession confirmation signal; under this regime, further labor deterioration should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined high-UNRATE/high-FEDFUNDS state does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the interaction term adds no value beyond UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth alongside rising retail sales identifies a broad demand expansion that supports earnings and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP growth and retail sales growth together fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal underperforms each proxy individually in out-of-sample tests.
